title: Repo list
blurb: The main two-pane dashboard: repos on the left, live log/diff on the right.

bindings.action: Scroll the list view (over the left pane)
bindings.note: web-app style — the selection stays put and may scroll out of view; keyboard nav always keeps the selection on screen

bindings.action: Move the selection (over the left pane), like ↑/↓

bindings.note: the Result summary row

bindings.action: Toggle the Result/Errors summary in the preview
bindings.note: any navigation clears it; on a folder/group header: collapse/expand
groups from ~/.config/polygit/groups.json; persisted
folders from recursive discovery; persisted; inert when every repo is at the scan root

bindings.action: Fold leader: toggle the selected folder/group
bindings.note: za toggle · zo/zc open/close · zO expand subtree · zM collapse all · zR expand all

bindings.action: Collapse the selected header, or jump to the enclosing folder/group
bindings.note: tree-style; also the clickable `←/` footer hint

bindings.action: Expand a collapsed folder/group (on its header)
bindings.note: also the clickable `→ fold` footer hint
command/url sources; re-resolves now, bypassing the cache TTL

bindings.action: Cycle focus across the visible panels: [1] list · [2] info · [3] result · [4] repo page
bindings.note: the focused panel gets a bright rounded border; hidden panels are skipped

bindings.action: Focus the list / info / result / repo-page panel directly

bindings.note: when panel [3] is focused
also clickable in the status bar; the divider grip fills solid while dragging
on a folder/collapsible group header: collapse/expand instead

bindings.action: Retry the selected repo if it failed or was skipped
bindings.note: with a folder/group header selected: retry every failed/skipped repo it covers (folder subtree, recursively)

bindings.action: Retry all repos with an issue

bindings.note: re-pull regardless of status — incl. idle/cached repos when auto-pull was suppressed; with a folder/group header selected, re-pulls every repo it covers (folder subtree, recursively)

bindings.action: Refetch / pull all repos not in progress
bindings.note: the manual "pull everything" when auto-pull-on-launch is off or suppressed

bindings.action: Refresh the selected repo's local git facts (no pull)
bindings.note: branch, ahead/behind, dirty, stash, branch count — network-free

bindings.action: Refresh all repos' local git facts (no pull)
Toggle the info panel above the log/diff
bindings.note: additive block; tracks the selection. Bold labels; empty rows hidden; clickable branch/commit/remote/pull-request links (branch & remote wrap on separators rather than truncate); the Pull Request row (#N title, via gh) shows when the current branch has an open PR; ⧉ copies the path (and the log, from the log pane); truncated path/subject expand on click

bindings.action: Toggle the result/log panel (the bottom of the preview)
bindings.note: info and result are independent panels in the right pane, split by a draggable boundary; hide the result panel and the info panel fills the pane (reads like the repo list). Persisted; clickable 'I log' footer hint

or click the [cols ▾] header chip for a dropdown. then u/a/d/l/w/b/s/p/c/r/f → status/ahead-behind/dirty/last/worktrees/branches/stashes/pulled/changed/pull-request/favorite; stays active until Esc. The status column shows a short label with the failure kind (not found / auth / diverged / timeout / network / lock) on failed repos. The pulled (⇣) and changed (±) columns show what the last pull landed — commits and files — hidden until a pull lands a delta, then they stay for the session (a retry won't flicker them). The pull-request column shows a clickable #N (via gh, cached 5 min) for the current branch's open PR. The favorite (★) column shows a clickable star per repo (★ favorited / ☆ not) — click to toggle. Zeros render dim in the Unicode set and are hidden in emoji mode; a column every repo lacks (no worktrees/stashes, ≤1 branch) auto-hides and its menu chip is dim/inert

or click the [sort ▾] header chip for a dropdown. then n/c/s/d (+a/l/w/b/k/p/g/r for visible columns) → name/branch/status/dirty/ahead-behind/last/worktrees/branches/stashes/pulled/changed/pull-request; the menu lists only currently-visible columns. Re-pick flips ▲▼ (or click a header); the status-bar ⟪column ▲⟫ tag is clickable too and flips direction. The list is always sorted — Name asc is the default; ties break by name (A→Z), regardless of the column's direction
then a/u/c/s/f/i → all/updated/up-to-date/skipped/failed/issues

bindings.action: Open the selected repo's remote in the browser
Copy the selected repo's absolute path
bindings.note: every copy confirms with a toast previewing the first lines of the copied text

bindings.action: Copy the selected repo's remote (origin) URL
Start claude code in the selected repo
bindings.note: suspends the TUI; PULL_CLAUDE_CMD overrides

bindings.action: Open lazygit in the selected repo
bindings.note: suspends the TUI; warns if lazygit is not installed
empties the streamed pull output

bindings.action: Open the documentation website in the browser

bindings.note: Sections: Lists · Theming · Sync · Interaction · Layout · Tooltips (master toggle + per-area: footer commands, column headers, group counts, settings rows, help links). A radio chip click sets that value (no cycle); the row label cycles. [x]/outside-click closes. Press R (or the R reset footer chip) to reset all settings to defaults — a confirmation lists each change as current → default

bindings.action: Open the Changelog (every release as an accordion, latest two expanded; j/k select, space/enter fold). A What's New modal also pops automatically after reloading into a newer build.
bindings.note: the clickable version tag in the status bar's bottom-right

Tab switches tabs (Hotkeys · CLI · Legend · Design · About). On the Design tab, v toggles flat ⇄ tabbed (vertical section tabs) and [/] move between sections; the preview-confirm button opens the shared dialog.

bindings.action: Fuzzy-filter repos by name (subsequence match, ranked by relevance; matched chars underlined; selection follows the first match, Enter keeps it)
Toggle the selected repo as a favorite (★)
Toggle the ★ Favorites section pinned to the top of the list
Open the fuzzy finder overlay — search every repo across all folders and jump to one
bindings.note: type to fuzzy-filter, ↑↓/PgUp/PgDn move, ^S cycles sort (relevance/name/recent/most-used), Enter jumps the list to that repo (recorded in the shared ~/.config/goto-repo/history), Esc closes

bindings.action: Open the folder picker to add a folder (or a single repo) to the workspace
bindings.note: breadcrumbs + ←/Backspace parent, type to fuzzy-filter, Enter opens a folder / selects a git repo, ^S selects the current folder, ^B bookmark, ^H home; the chosen folder is scanned and persisted

bindings.action: Remove the selected repo's (or folder header's) folder from the workspace
bindings.note: drops the root and hides its repos; persisted to the active named workspace (`-w`), session-only otherwise; re-add via the picker

bindings.action: Clear the filter and restore the pre-filter selection, or quit when no filter

bindings.note: with a modal open it closes the modal instead (the footer hint reads `q close`); `, settings` / `? help` / `q` stay clickable from inside any modal
exit code 130
only while the top-right `↺ new build installed` notice is up; restores the terminal and exec's the new binary with the same args (also the clickable `[^R reload]`); works from any view or modal
only while the notice is up; re-arms if the binary changes again (also the clickable `[^X]`); works from any view or modal

title: Repo page
blurb: Detail view of one repo (panel [4], restored by default — a docked bottom panel — or maximized full-screen with m): every local branch (with change stats vs the base branch), worktree, and stash, with fresh ahead/behind and a bottom info panel.

bindings.note: stash, dirty row, or a branch vs its base; "no changes" toasts instead
clean, non-current branch

or click the [cols ▾] / [sort ▾] title-bar chips for dropdowns. then b/y/a/m/d/c/u/f/g/r/s → ahead-behind/dirty/added/modified/deleted/total/upstream/age/pr/subject (pr = the current branch's open #N, clickable, via gh); clickable chips; empty columns auto-hide and their chip is dim/inert; persisted. Stash rows render the same change-stat columns (added/modified/deleted/total), loaded lazily from git stash show
branch/upstream/base + merge-base, ahead-behind, change stats, last commit; persisted

bindings.action: Context action: delete branch / drop stash / remove worktree / discard
bindings.note: dynamic to the row; confirmation scaled to danger

bindings.action: Open the selected branch on the remote (e.g. GitHub) in the browser
Copy menu — absolute path / branch name / both
bindings.note: copying confirms with a toast previewing the copied text

bindings.action: Base-branch picker for the selected branch
bindings.note: overrides which base its diff stats compare against; click the base cell too. Pick "auto-detect" to clear the override; persisted

bindings.action: Start claude code in the row's path
Open lazygit in the row's path

bindings.note: restored = docked panel [4]; maximized = full-screen. Also the ▢/▣ title-bar button (left of [esc back]) and Settings → Layout → Repo page; the state is sticky (persisted)

title: Diff modal
blurb: A 90%-of-screen overlay: a file-list panel over the selected file’s diff, in two bordered sub-panels.

bindings.action: Switch focus between the file list and the diff
bindings.note: the focused panel gets a bright border
pick a file, or scroll the diff

bindings.action: First/last file, or diff top/bottom (per focus)

bindings.note: Alt too — moves the selection a viewport at a time
Alt+wheel too — selection unchanged

bindings.action: Filter the file list by status
bindings.note: shown with >10 files across ≥2 statuses; cycles all → each status → all; clickable chips with count badges, list grouped by status
dirty rows only; re-lists files

bindings.action: Discard changes (current branch) / remove worktree / drop stash
bindings.note: with confirmation
